chapter_title.ar: بَابُ حَجِّ الْمُجَاوِرِينَ وَ قُطَّانِ مَكَّةَ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 57 – The Hajj of the ones in the vicinity and residents of Makkah

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح على الظاهر - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/207)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح على الظاهر - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/207)
text: كُنْتُ مُجَاوِراً بِمَكَّةَ فَسَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) مِنْ أَيْنَ أُحْرِمُ بِالْحَجِّ فَقَالَ مِنْ حَيْثُ أَحْرَمَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) مِنَ الْجِعْرَانَةِ أَتَاهُ فِي ذَلِكَ الْمَكَانِ فُتُوحٌ فَتْحُ الطَّائِفِ وَ فَتْحُ خَيْبَرَ وَ الْفَتْحُ فَقُلْتُ مَتَى أَخْرُجُ قَالَ إِنْ كُنْتَ صَرُورَةً فَإِذَا مَضَى مِنْ ذِي الْحِجَّةِ يَوْمٌ وَ إِنْ كُنْتَ قَدْ حَجَجْتَ قَبْلَ ذَلِكَ فَإِذَا مَضَى مِنَ الشَّهْرِ خَمْسٌ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Husayn Bin Saeed, from Safwan, from Abu Al Fazl who said,

‘I was in the vicinity of Makkah, so I asked Abu Abdullahasws: ‘From where should I be wearing the Ihraam for the Hajj. So heasws said: ‘From where Rasool-Allahsaww wore hissaww Ihraam, from Al-Ji’rana. Victories came to himsaww in that place – the victory of Al-Taif, and victory of Khaybar, and the (other) victories’. So I said, ‘When shall I go out?’ Heasws said: ‘If you were a first time Pilgrim, so when a day from Zil-Hijja passes by, and if you have performed Hajj before that, so when five (days) pass by from the month’.
en.sarwar: 9. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from al-Husayn ibn Sa‘id from Safwan from abu al-Fadl who has said the following: “I resided in Makkah and I asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about wherefrom should I assume the state of Ihram for al-Hajj. He (the Imam) said, ‘You must assume the state of Ihram from where the Messenger of Allah, O Allah, grant compensation to Muhammad and his family worthy of their services to Your cause, did which is al-Ja‘ranah, which is the place where victory came to him several times like the victory of Ta’if, Khaybar and the Victory.’ I then asked, ‘When should I go out?’ He (the Imam) said, ‘If it is your first al-Hajj, you can go out on the second day of Dhu al-Hajj ah, but if it is not your first al-Hajj, then you can go out on fifth of Dhu al-Hajj ah.’”
